entities.name: PIGEONPEA
entities.description: A legume crop whose yield potential is constrained by various diseases.

entities.name: FUSARIUM WILT
entities.description: A major disease constraint to pigeonpea production worldwide.

entities.name: STERILITY MOSAIC DISEASE
entities.description: A major disease constraint to pigeonpea production worldwide.

entities.name: PHYTOPHTHORA BLIGHT
entities.description: An emerging potential threat to pigeonpea production.

entities.name: ALTERNARIA BLIGHT
entities.description: An emerging potential threat to pigeonpea production.

entities.name: HIGH YIELD POTENTIAL
entities.description: The desired productivity level of improved pigeonpea genotypes.

entities.name: HIGH THROUGHPUT PHENOTYPING
entities.description: A method used for the identification of host plant resistance to diseases.

entities.name: HOST PLANT RESISTANCE
entities.description: The ability of a plant to resist disease, used for disease management.

entities.name: SCREENING TECHNIQUES
entities.description: Stepwise evaluation methods developed at ICRISAT to identify resistant sources.

entities.name: ICRISAT
entities.description: The organization where screening techniques for disease resistance were developed.

entities.name: BIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of the living organisms causing pigeonpea diseases.

entities.name: EPIDEMIOLOGY
entities.description: The study of how pigeonpea diseases spread and are distributed.

entities.name: MANAGEMENT METHODS
entities.description: Techniques used to control and manage pigeonpea diseases.
